title: dipsacus fullonum subsp. sativus
intro: Dipsacus fullonum subsp. sativus, also known as Fuller's teasel, is a plant species within the teasel family (Dipsacaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Fuller's teasel include its ability to soothe and calm respiratory tract irritations, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Dipsacus fullonum subsp. sativus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate related symptoms.
